questions.question: Let z = 2 + 3i. Find z² and express your answer in the form a + bi.
Compute i^47 and express your answer as a single power of i or a real number.
questions.explanation: Since powers of i repeat with period 4: i^47 = i^(44+3) = (i^4)^11 · i^3 = 1^11 · i^3 = i^3 = −i.

questions.question: If (3 + 4i)(a + bi) = 7 + i for real numbers a and b, find a + b.
questions.explanation: Expand: 3a − 4b + (4a + 3b)i = 7 + i. Then 3a − 4b = 7 and 4a + 3b = 1. Solving: a = 1, b = −1, so a + b = 0.

questions.question: Let z = 1 + i. Find 1/z and express your answer in the form a + bi.
questions.explanation: Multiply numerator and denominator by the conjugate: 1/(1+i) · (1−i)/(1−i) = (1−i)/(1−i²) = (1−i)/2.

questions.question: If |z| = 5 and z = 3 + bi where b is real and positive, find b.
questions.explanation: |z|² = 3² + b² = 25, so b² = 16. Since b > 0, b = 4.

questions.question: Compute (1 + i)^4 and express your answer as a single real number or in the form a + bi.
